You are updating "The Shaw Report" — an internal weekly financial briefing for Jackson Shaw, a Dallas-based commercial real estate developer. Today is Friday. Your job is to update two JavaScript data files with real, current information for this week's brief.

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Use WebSearch and WebFetch to research real current data before writing anything
- Only update friday-finance-data.js and tenant-lender-data.js
- Do NOT modify index.html, app.js, or styles.css
- All data must be accurate — do not fabricate numbers or headlines
- Write the complete updated file contents, not just diffs

=============================================================
STEP 1 — DETERMINE THIS WEEK'S DATE
=============================================================
Use today's date. The "date" field should be formatted like "April 4, 2025" and "date_short" like "Apr 4, 2025".

=============================================================
STEP 2 — UPDATE friday-finance-data.js
=============================================================

FILE LOCATION: /home/user/Shaw-Business/friday-finance-data.js

Read the current file first, then update EVERY section with real data for this week.

--- TICKER BAR (ticker_bar array) ---
11 items. Each has: { label, value, change, up: true/false }
Search for current week's closing values for:
1. S&P 500 — label "S&P 500", ticker SPX
2. Dow Jones — label "Dow", ticker DJIA
3. NASDAQ — label "NASDAQ", ticker IXIC
4. 10-Year Treasury yield — label "10Y Treasury"
5. 2-Year Treasury yield — label "2Y Treasury"
6. Fed Funds Rate (EFFR) — label "Fed Funds"
7. 1-Month SOFR — label "1M SOFR"
8. DSCR/bridge loan rate proxy — label "Avg DSCR Rate" (estimate based on 10Y + ~175bps spread)
9. WTI Crude Oil — label "WTI Crude"
10. Gold — label "Gold"
11. US Dollar Index (DXY) — label "DXY"

For change fields: use week-over-week change (e.g., "+12.4 pts", "+0.08%", "-$1.20"). Set up: true if positive for markets (stocks up, rates down for CRE context, oil movement).

--- NEWS BULLETS (news_bullets array) ---
5 items. Each has: { text } — HTML allowed (use <b> for key terms/numbers).
Research the top 5 market-moving stories this week relevant to CRE and finance:
- Fed decisions or Fed speakers
- Economic data (CPI, jobs, GDP, housing)
- Treasury market moves
- Trade policy / tariffs
- Banking / credit market news
Keep each bullet to 1-2 sentences. Bold the key number or fact.

--- WATCH NEXT WEEK (watch_next_week string) ---
Single paragraph. HTML allowed. Research what economic data releases and Fed events are scheduled for next week. Bold the key events (e.g., <b>CPI</b>, <b>FOMC minutes</b>).

--- READING LINKS (reading_links array) ---
5 items. Each has: { title, url, source, description }
Find 5 non-paywalled articles published THIS WEEK relevant to:
- Commercial real estate trends
- Interest rates / Fed policy
- Industrial real estate / logistics
- Capital markets / lending
- Economic outlook

Preferred sources (non-paywalled): CNBC, Reuters, Bloomberg (some free), CoStar News, Bisnow, The Real Deal, GlobeSt, Commercial Observer, Axios, Bankrate, Mortgage Bankers Association, CBRE Insights, JLL Research. Avoid WSJ, FT, Barron's (paywalled).

--- KEY RATES (rates object) ---
4 rows. Each has: { label, current, one_month_ago, one_year_ago }
1. Fed Funds (EFFR) — upper bound of target range, format "X.XX%"
2. 10Y Treasury — format "X.XX%"
3. 1M SOFR — format "X.XX%"
4. Prime Rate — always Fed Funds + 3.00%, format "X.XX%"

For one_month_ago and one_year_ago: use approximate values based on historical data you know or can search.

--- STOCKS (stocks object) ---
This is a large object with 4 categories. READ the current file to get the full list of tickers and companies — do NOT change the ticker list, just update the data.

Structure: stocks: { indexes: [...], cre_reits: [...], retail_tenants: [...], lenders: [...] }
Each entry: { company, ticker, price, day_pct, ytd_pct, high_52w, low_52w }

Search Yahoo Finance or similar for current week closing prices. Use real data.
- price: number (e.g., 4521.30)
- day_pct: string with sign (e.g., "+1.24%", "-0.87%")
- ytd_pct: string with sign
- high_52w: number
- low_52w: number

For INDEX entries (SPX, DJIA, IXIC, RUT): use index point values, not dollar amounts.

=============================================================
STEP 3 — UPDATE tenant-lender-data.js
=============================================================

FILE LOCATION: /home/user/Shaw-Business/tenant-lender-data.js

Read the current file to see the full list of 23 monitored companies.

MONITORED COMPANIES (do not add or remove any):
Tenants: Lincoln Tech, Crown Equipment, Tongrun International, Canteen, Control Panels, Baer Manufacturing / Hunt Electric, Grander Distribution, Clopay, International Paper, Duroshox, Bright Events, Xingyu Lighting, Hartford Insurance, Triad Manufacturing
Lenders: Busey Bank, Huntington Bank, Fifth Third Bank, Inwood National Bank, Simmons Bank, Barings, Hall Group, PACE Equities
Equity Partner: Sammons Enterprise

For EACH company, search: "[Company Name] news 2025 2026" and "[Company Name] earnings bankruptcy layoffs"

Update any alerts where NEW significant developments have occurred since the last scan.
Keep existing alerts if still relevant and no update needed.
Add new alerts for any significant developments found.
Remove or downgrade alerts that are no longer relevant.

Alert levels:
- HIGH: Bankruptcy filing, payment default, fraud, major legal action, facility closures affecting lease
- MEDIUM: Credit downgrade, C-suite departure, earnings miss >10%, major regulatory action
- LOW: Minor litigation, restructuring warning, analyst price target cut, acquisition integration risk
- GENERAL: Positive news, expansions, record earnings, new leadership (positive)

Each alert: { level, company, cat, headline, summary (HTML ok, use <b>), relevance, source, date }

Update: date to today, last_scan to today's ISO timestamp.

=============================================================
STEP 4 — WRITE THE UPDATED FILES
=============================================================

After completing your research:

1. Read /home/user/Shaw-Business/friday-finance-data.js to get the exact file structure
2. Read /home/user/Shaw-Business/tenant-lender-data.js to get the exact file structure
3. Write the complete updated friday-finance-data.js with all new data
4. Write the complete updated tenant-lender-data.js with all new data

Preserve the exact JavaScript variable names (FRIDAY_FINANCE and TENANT_LENDER_NEWS) and file structure. Only change the data values, dates, and content — not the structure.

=============================================================
VERIFICATION
=============================================================

After writing both files, confirm:
- [ ] friday-finance-data.js date matches today's Friday date
- [ ] All 11 ticker_bar items have current values
- [ ] 5 news_bullets written with real this-week stories
- [ ] 5 reading_links are non-paywalled and published this week
- [ ] All stock prices updated with real data
- [ ] tenant-lender-data.js last_scan updated to today
- [ ] At least a scan pass done for HIGH/MEDIUM alert companies
